name: Transform CHOP
description: The Transform CHOP takes transformations in various formats, applied operations to them, and outputs them in various formats. It can be used to:
summary: The Transform CHOP takes transformations in various formats, applied operations to them, and outputs them in various formats. It can be used to:

parameters.name: Custom Input Orders
parameters.label: Custom Input Orders
parameters.description: Parameter of this operator. See operator description for details.

parameters.name: Transform Order
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description:
inxord -  - Changing the Transform order will change where things go much the same way as going a block and turning east gets you to a different place than turning east and then going a block. In matrix math terms, if we use the 'multiply vector on the right' (column vector) convention, a transform order of Scale, Rotate, Translate would be written as T * R * S * Position

 srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.name: Scale Rotate Translate
parameters.label: Scale Rotate Translate
parameters.description: srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.name: Rotate Order
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description:
inrord -  - As with transform order (above), changing the order in which the rotations take place will alter the final position and orientation. A Rotation order of Rx Ry Rz would create the final rotation matrix as follows R = Rz * Ry * Rx

 xyz - xzy - yxz - yzx - zxy - zyx -

parameters.name: Rx Ry Rz
parameters.label: Rx Ry Rz
parameters.description: xyz - xzy - yxz - yzx - zxy - zyx -

parameters.name: Input 0 Pre Operation
parameters.label: Input 0 Pre Operation
parameters.description:
input0preop -  - Operation(s) to apply on the transforms on Input 0, before they are combined with other transforms.

 none - No operation is applied. invert - Invert the transform. transpose - Transpose the transform. This only has an effect for matrix format transforms. inverttranspose - Invert and Transpose the transform. The transpose will only be done if the input is a matrix format transform.

parameters.name: None
parameters.label: None
parameters.description: none - No operation is applied. invert - Invert the transform. transpose - Transpose the transform. This only has an effect for matrix format transforms. inverttranspose - Invert and Transpose the transform. The transpose will only be done if the input is a matrix format transform.

parameters.name: Input 1 Pre Operation
parameters.label: Input 1 Pre Operation
parameters.description:
input1preop -  - Operation(s) to apply on the transforms on Input 1, before they are combined with other transforms.

 none - No operation is applied. invert - Invert the transform. transpose - Transpose the transform. This only has an effect for matrix format transforms. inverttranspose - Invert and Transpose the transform. The transpose will only be done if the input is a matrix format transform.

parameters.name: Input Operation
parameters.label: Input Operation
parameters.description:
inputoperation -  - The operation that should be applied between transforms coming from Input 0 and Input 1. Refer to the main description of this node for an explanation of how multiple samples and/or transform sets are combined between the two inputs.

 input0input1 - The operation will be input1 * input0. It it ordered this way since ultimately the transform is applied to a position/vector in the form input1 * input0 * Position, so the input0 operation is done first. input1input0 - The operation will be input1 * input0. quatlerp - quatslerp -

parameters.name: Input 0, then Input 1
parameters.label: Input 0, then Input 1
parameters.description: input0input1 - The operation will be input1 * input0. It it ordered this way since ultimately the transform is applied to a position/vector in the form input1 * input0 * Position, so the input0 operation is done first. input1input0 - The operation will be input1 * input0. quatlerp - quatslerp -

parameters.name: Input Weight
parameters.label: Input Weight
parameters.description: inputweight - Specify the Blend weight for the above 'Input Operation'

parameters.name: Translate
parameters.label: Translate
parameters.description:
t -  - XYZ translation values.

 tx - ty - tz -

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: tx - ty - tz -

parameters.name: Rotate
parameters.label: Rotate
parameters.description:
r -  - XYZ rotation, in degrees.

 rx - ry - rz -

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
s -  - XYZ scale to shrink or enlarge the transform.

 sx - sy - sz -

parameters.name: Pivot
parameters.label: Pivot
parameters.description:
p -  - XYZ pivot to apply the above operations around.

 px - py - pz -

parameters.name: Xform Matrix/CHOP/DAT
parameters.label: Xform Matrix/CHOP/DAT
parameters.description: xformmatrixop -

parameters.name: Multiply Order
parameters.label: Multiply Order
parameters.description:
multiplyorder -  - Controls how the input transform(s) are combined with the transform specified on this page. The below two descriptions use a multiply "vector on the right" convention (column vectors).

 inputxformpage - The transforms will be combined as Transform Page * Input. xformpageinput - The transforms will be combined as Input * Transform Page.

parameters.name: Input, then Transform Page
parameters.label: Input, then Transform Page
parameters.description: inputxformpage - The transforms will be combined as Transform Page * Input. xformpageinput - The transforms will be combined as Input * Transform Page.

parameters.name: Look At
parameters.label: Look At
parameters.description: Allows you to generate your transform channels by specifying an object you would like to Look At, or point to.

parameters.name: Up Vector
parameters.label: Up Vector
parameters.description:
upvector -  - When orienting an object, the Up Vector is used to determine where the positive Y axis points.

 upvectorx - upvectory - upvectorz -

parameters.name: Forward Direction
parameters.label: Forward Direction
parameters.description:
forwarddir -  - Sets which axis and direction is considered the forward direction.

 posx - negx - posy - negy - posz - negz -

parameters.name: +X
parameters.label: +X
parameters.description: posx - negx - posy - negy - posz - negz -

parameters.name: Pre Operation
parameters.label: Pre Operation
parameters.description:
preop -  - Operation(s) to apply on the transform generated by Transform/Rotate/Scale/Pivot parameters, before it is combined with other transforms. Done before combining with the Xform Matrix and Look At.

 none - No operation. invert - Invert the transform.

parameters.name: Operation
parameters.label: Operation
parameters.description:
operation -  - Controls how the input transform(s) are combined with the transform specified on this page. The below two descriptions use a multiply "vector on the right" convention (column vectors).

 inputxformpage - xformpageinput - quatlerp - quatslerp -

parameters.name: Weight
parameters.label: Weight
parameters.description: weight - Specify the Blend weight for the above 'Operation'

parameters.name: Post Operation
parameters.label: Post Operation
parameters.description:
postop -  - Optionally applied one last operation to the final generated transform before it is output.

 none - No operation. invert - Invert the transform. transpose - Transpose the transform. This only has an effect for matrix format transforms. inverttranspose - Invert and Transpose the transform. The transpose will only be done if the input is a matrix format transform.

parameters.name: Output
parameters.label: Output
parameters.description:
output -  - Specify the format the transform will be output in.

 transform - The standard Translate, Rotate and Scale channels. t[xyz], r[xyz], s[xyz]. Will also include xord and rord channels for Transform and Rotate Order, unless 'Include Order Channels' is turned off. transformquat - Translate, Quaternion (for rotation) and Scale channels. t[xyz], q[xyzw], s[xyz]. Will also include xord channel for Transform Order, unless 'Include Order Channels' is turned off. mat - 16 channels for a 4x4 matrix. The channels will be output in column-by-column order. That is, with the last 4 channels being the 'translate' portion of the matrix. mat3 - 9 channels for a 3x3 matrix. This includes the rotation and the scale, but not the translation. position - The final position of the transform in space. This doesn't include any orientation information.

parameters.name: Transform (Euler)
parameters.label: Transform (Euler)
parameters.description: transform - The standard Translate, Rotate and Scale channels. t[xyz], r[xyz], s[xyz]. Will also include xord and rord channels for Transform and Rotate Order, unless 'Include Order Channels' is turned off. transformquat - Translate, Quaternion (for rotation) and Scale channels. t[xyz], q[xyzw], s[xyz]. Will also include xord channel for Transform Order, unless 'Include Order Channels' is turned off. mat - 16 channels for a 4x4 matrix. The channels will be output in column-by-column order. That is, with the last 4 channels being the 'translate' portion of the matrix. mat3 - 9 channels for a 3x3 matrix. This includes the rotation and the scale, but not the translation. position - The final position of the transform in space. This doesn't include any orientation information.

parameters.name: Determinant
parameters.label: Determinant
parameters.description: determ - When outputting a matrix, it's determinant can also be output by enabling this parameter.

parameters.name: Un-matched Channels
parameters.label: Un-matched Channels
parameters.description:
unmatchedchans -  - Controls how channels that don't match the naming convention for the various transform format are treated.

 warn - Give a warning if transform channels that don't match any of the naming convenstions are found. ignore - Ignore (give no warning) if channels that don't match the naming convention are found. delete - Delete all channels that don't match any of the naming conventions.

parameters.name: Warn
parameters.label: Warn
parameters.description: warn - Give a warning if transform channels that don't match any of the naming convenstions are found. ignore - Ignore (give no warning) if channels that don't match the naming convention are found. delete - Delete all channels that don't match any of the naming conventions.

parameters.name: Custom Output Orders
parameters.label: Custom Output Orders
parameters.description: custoutputorders - By default the output transforms will use the orders given on teh Transform page. Enabling this allows for custom orders to be used for the transform that is output. This doesn't change the transform itself, but the values of the channels will likely change since they are combined in a different order to obtain the same overall transform.

parameters.name: Include Order Channels
parameters.label: Include Order Channels
parameters.description: includeorderchans - Specified if the 'xord' and 'rord' channels should be output from this node. 'xord' will be output for 'Transform (Euler)' and 'Transform (Quaternion)' modes. 'rord' will be output for the 'Transform (Euler)' mode. The matrix and position modes do not include orders.

parameters.name: Continuous Rotations
parameters.label: Continuous Rotations
parameters.description: continuousrotations - In the case the input has multiple samples, this will attempt to keep rotations of neighbouring samples continuous. Basically, it tries to avoid 360 degree jumps. 360-> 361 instead of 360 -> 1 (which is the same two rotations.

parameters.name: Use Rotation Hint
parameters.label: Use Rotation Hint
parameters.description: usehint - An initial rotation hint given in r[xyz] degrees to try to stay continuous against. Turning this on and using the  parameter below allows you to specify approximate starting values for the rotation channels produced. This allows you to change the rotation channel solution to a specific starting point (e.g. for camera output control).

parameters.name: Hint
parameters.label: Hint
parameters.description:
hint -  - Specify approximate starting values for the rotation channels produced.

 hintx - hinty - hintz -

parameters.name: Time Slice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: timeslice - Turning this on forces the channels to be "Time Sliced".  A  is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: Scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: scope - To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a  string on the Common page. See Pattern Matching.

parameters.name: Sample Rate Match
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description:
srselect -  - Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Options are not available.

 first - Use rate of first input to resample others. max - Resample to the highest sample rate. min - Resample to the lowest sample rate. err - Doesn't accept conflicting sample rates.

parameters.name: Resample At First Input's Rate
parameters.label: Resample At First Input's Rate
parameters.description: first - Use rate of first input to resample others. max - Resample to the highest sample rate. min - Resample to the lowest sample rate. err - Doesn't accept conflicting sample rates.

parameters.name: Export Method
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description:
exportmethod -  - This will determine how to connect the  channel to the parameter. Refer to the Export article for more information.

 datindex - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the index of the channel in the . datname - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the name of the channel in the . autoname - The channel is the full destination of where to export to, such has geo1/transform1:tx.

parameters.name: DAT Table by Index
parameters.label: DAT Table by Index
parameters.description: datindex - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the index of the channel in the . datname - Uses the docked  table and references the channel via the name of the channel in the . autoname - The channel is the full destination of where to export to, such has geo1/transform1:tx.

parameters.name: Export Root
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: autoexportroot - This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by Channel Name is : are relative to.

parameters.name: Export Table
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: exporttable - The  used to hold the export information when using the  Table  Methods (See above).
